title: 10 best herbal tinctures for eye twitching

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with eye twitching because of its unique ability to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle spasms. The active compounds in valerian root, such as isovaleric acid and valerenic acid, have a sedative effect on the body, which can help to relax the facial muscles that control eye movement. This relaxation can help to reduce the frequency and severity of eye twitching, providing relief from the discomfort and distraction caused by this common condition.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Relaxant Properties: Valerian tincture's sedative and relaxant properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing muscle spasms and twitching associated with eye twitching.
Muscle Relaxant Effects: Valerian's muscle relaxant effects can directly target the muscles surrounding the eyes, helping to reduce tension and spasms that contribute to eye twitching.
Antispasmodic Properties: Valerian tincture's antispasmodic properties help to relieve involuntary contractions of the muscles, including those around the eyes, which can contribute to eye twitching.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This sesquiterpene acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help reduce muscle spasms, including eye twitching, by promoting relaxation and reducing neuronal excitability.
Valeranone: This valerenic acid derivative has sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help alleviate eye twitching by reducing stress and promoting a calm state.

Acidic phenolic compounds (e.g., valeranone-7-acetate): These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, potentially alleviating eye twitching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are rich in valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for its sedative and relaxing properties, which help alleviate eye twitching.
Leaves: Valeriana officinalis leaves contain a high concentration of valerenic acid and other flavonoids, making them a valuable part for tincture production to soothe eye twitching.

Stems: The stems of Valeriana officinalis also contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its use in tinctures for eye twitching, providing a natural remedy for spasmodic eye movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes by digging them up in autumn after the first frost.
Chop 2 parts of apple cider vinegar and 1 part of distilled water to make a solvent.
Combine 1 part of the chopped valeriana officinalis with 2 parts of the apple cider vinegar solution and 1 part of distilled water.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture and transfer it to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with eye twitching because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a herb that has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle spasms. The antispasmodic properties of maypop help to relax the eyelid muscles, reducing twitching and spasms. Additionally, maypop's calming effects on the nervous system can also alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers for eye twitching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, maypop tinctures offer a natural remedy for soothing restless eyes and banishing eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Passiflora incarnata (Maypop): Contains flavonoids and glycosides that have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm muscle spasms and reduce eye twitching.
Papaverin: Present in Maypop tinctures, papaverin is a natural smooth muscle relaxant that can help to alleviate spasms and twitching of the eyelid muscles.
Tannins: Maypop contains high amounts of tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and irritation that may be contributing to eye twitching.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: Harmane is an alkaloid found in Passiflora incarnata, which may help alleviate eye twitching by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms.

Rotundifuran: Rotundifuran is a flavonoid present in Passiflora incarnata, which may help with eye twitching by exhibiting antioxidant properties, reducing inflammation, and modulating the nervous system to prevent spasms.

Isovitexin: Isovitexin is a flavonoid glycoside found in Passiflora incarnata, which may help alleviate eye twitching by acting as a potent antioxidant, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating the nervous system to prevent muscle spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make tinctures for eye twitching due to their high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have sedative and antispasmodic properties.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for eye twitching due to their content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to calm and relax the nervous system.
Fruits: They are used to make tinctures for eye twitching due to their high antioxidant content, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 4 hours.
Combine 1 part dried passiflora incarnata with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar to create the tincture.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it every day for 2 weeks to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids after 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tinctures helps with eye twitching because it contains a compound called baicalin, which has been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a tincture, skullcap's active compounds can help to soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing muscle tension that may be contributing to the twitching. Additionally, skullcap's adaptogenic qualities allow it to promote relaxation and reduce stress, common triggers of eye twitching. By addressing these underlying causes, skullcap tinctures can provide natural relief from persistent eye twitching.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Skullcap's anxiolytic properties can help to calm anxiety and stress, which are common underlying causes of eye twitching, by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.

Muscle relaxant properties: The flavonoids present in skullcap tincture, such as baicalin and wogonin, have been shown to possess muscle-relaxant effects, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and twitches in the eye area.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Skullcap's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to eye twitching and other symptoms like dryness or itchiness.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides, a group of triterpenoid saponins, help alleviate eye twitching by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, thus soothing the muscles around the eyes.
Scutellarin: Scutellarin, a flavonoid glycoside, exhibits antispasmodic properties, which can help calm muscle spasms and reduce the frequency of eye twitching.
Baicalein: Baicalein, a flavonoid compound, has anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce stress-related eye twitching and promote overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to relax and soothe eye muscles.
Roots: They have been traditionally used to calm and reduce spasms in the eyes.
Flowers: They are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the scutellaria lateriflora plant using clean scissors or clippers to avoid contamination.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and measure out 100 grams of the plant.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of 80 proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four weeks to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle after four weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with eye twitching because it possesses a calming and soothing effect on the body, which can help to reduce muscle tension and spasms. The natural antispasmodic properties of lavender work to relax the eyelid muscles, alleviating twitching and spasms. Additionally, lavender's ability to calm the nervous system can also help to ease stress and anxiety, common triggers for eye twitching. Muscle Relaxant: The antispasmodic compounds in lavender can directly relax muscles, including those around the eyes, helping to alleviate twitching and spasms.

Anti-Inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and irritation in the eye area, which can contribute to twitching, and also alleviate any related discomfort or pain.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as a natural sedative and muscle relaxant, helping to reduce muscle spasms and twitching associated with eye twitching.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene also contributes to the sedative and relaxing properties of lavender, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that may contribute to eye twitching.

remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their calming and relaxing properties, which can help alleviate eye twitching caused by stress and anxiety.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and spasms in the eyes.
Stems: The stems are used for their similar anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties to the leaves, which can help soothe and calm the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning when they are fully open and dry them immediately.
Chop the dried flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife or a pair of scissors.
Combine 250 grams of the chopped flowers with 750 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves and flowers from a clean and pesticide-free environment.
Dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area at 50c for 2 hours or until it reaches 8% moisture.
Use 200g of the dried plant material and add it to a glass jar along with 1l of 40% ethanol.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it gently for 2 weeks or 14 days.
Filter the tincture through a cheesecloth and transfer it to a clean glass bottle for storage.

Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking to enhance extraction.

Chop 1 part of melissa officinalis plant material in a food processor to release its essential oils.
Steep 1 part of chopped plant material in 2 parts of 80-proof vodka for 2-6 weeks in a glass jar.
Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to.
Store the melissa officinalis tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years in a glass bottle with.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinchona pubescens are commonly used to make tinctures for eye twitching due to their high concentration of quinine and other alkaloids that have a calming effect on the nervous system.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of cinchona pubescens bark in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the bark in a warm place at 40-50 degrees celsius for 24 hours to remove moisture.
Chop the dried bark into small pieces and combine with 75% ethanol to create a 1:3 ratio tincture.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass bottle for 2 weeks, shaking it every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with eye twitching because of its unique composition, which includes flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the muscles around the eyes, alleviating spasms and contractions that cause twitching. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation that may exacerbate eye twitching.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and oxygenation to the eyes, which can help alleviate spasms and twitches caused by decreased blood flow.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's terpenes and flavonoids have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and inflammation in the eyes that may be contributing to twitching or spasms.

Terpenoids: Compounds like bilobalide and ginkgolides may help calm nerve activity and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to eye twitching.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are widely used to make tinctures due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which are believed to help alleviate eye twitching symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of bilobalide, a compound that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye twitching.
Roots: The roots of Ginkgo biloba are sometimes used to make tinctures due to their rich content of ginkgolides, which may help alleviate eye twitching by improving blood circulation to the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 4-6 parts of ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers and dry them thoroughly in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks.
Combine 1 part of the dried ginkgo biloba with 2 parts of 80% vodka and 2 parts of glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 10 minutes each time.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, squeezing the solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
Bottle the tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and berries in their peak ripeness typically within 1 to 2 weeks.
Dry the harvested flowers and berries in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part dried sambucus nigra plant material with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass container.
Seal the container and steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with eye twitching because of its calming and soothing properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried ensuring maximum potency.

Combine 10 grams of dried or 20 grams of fresh verbena officinalis with 100 milliliters of vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for four weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and blending of plant constituents.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids after 48 hours of settling.

Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 14-21 days, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate extraction.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality cannabis sativa leaves ensuring potency and efficacy.
Combine one part dried cannabis sativa with two parts apple cider vinegar in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for two weeks shaking daily allowing infusions to occur.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass dropper bottles storing in refrigerator for up to six months.

Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Combine 50 grams of ground foeniculum vulgare with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure efficient extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and using the resulting liquid as desired dosage.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in quantities of 250 grams and store them in a cool dry place.
Combine the dried roots with 80% proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of one part roots to two parts vodka.
Seal the jar tightly and allow it to steep for six weeks in a dark cupboard or pantry.
Strain the liquid from the jar through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Allow the tincture to mature for at least four weeks before using it as directed for eye twitching relief.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for eye twitching are liquid extracts made from plants, which help alleviate and soothe eye twitching by reducing muscle spasms and calming the nervous system. The best herbal tinctures for eye twitching include Passiflora incarnata, Valeriana officinalis, Avena sativa, Lavandula angustifolia, Melissa officinalis, Scutellaria baicalensis, Ginkgo biloba, Hypericum perforatum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Zingiber officinale. If left untreated, eye twitching can disrupt sleep, cause eye strain, and impair daily activities, affecting work performance, social interactions, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye twitching that are treated with herbal tinctures are various nervous system disorders. For instance, tinnitus (ringing in the ears) is often addressed using calming herbs like valerian root and passionflower. Similarly, restless leg syndrome, characterized by involuntary movements or sensations in the legs, can be soothed by herbal blends containing chamomile and lavender. Additionally, herbal tinctures may also be used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, which can manifest as twitching or spasms in various parts of the body.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with eye twitching is a blend of Passiflora, Valerian root, and Eyebright. Passiflora is known for its calming effects, while Valerian root helps to relax the nervous system and reduce anxiety. Eyebright, with its anti-inflammatory properties, soothes and calms the eyes. Taking these tinctures in a 1:1:1 ratio, 2-3 times a day, can help alleviate eye twitching symptoms. Consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you have underlying medical conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tinctures helps with eye twitching because it contains a unique blend of active compounds that work to calm and soothe the nervous system. The herbal extract is known to have a sedative effect, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that often triggers eye twitching. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata has a natural anti-inflammatory property that may help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from twitching and spasms. Regular use of Passiflora incarnata tincture may help to alleviate eye twitching naturally.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to eye twitching by soothing the nerves and muscles around the eyes.
Alkaloids (specifically, harmine): Harmine has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help calm down excessive muscle contractions and spasms that cause eye twitching.
Glycosides: These compounds have a sedative and anti-anxiety effect, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can lead to eye twitching by promoting relaxation and calmness in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Passiflora incarnata contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to their anti-anxiety and sedative properties, helping to alleviate eye twitching.
Fruits: The fruits of Passiflora incarnata contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including eye twitching and anxiety.
Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been used to treat nervous system disorders, including eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part passiflora incarnata flowers and 2 parts organic vodka in a clean glass jar for tincture preparation.
Fill the jar to the top with 80 proof vodka and ensure the herb is completely covered for infusion.
Seal the jar and place it in a cool dark location for 2 to 6 weeks to allow infusion.
After 2 to 6 weeks strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a separate clean glass container to filter.
Discard the solids and store the passiflora incarnata tincture in a dark glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tinctures helps with eye twitching because of its calming effects on the nervous system, which can help alleviate spasms and muscle contractions in the eyelid. Roots: The roots also contain valeranone, which has been shown to have a relaxing effect on muscles and nerves, potentially alleviating eye twitching.
Roots: Additionally, the roots of Valeriana officinalis contain isovaleric acid, a compound that may help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to eye twitching.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part dried root of valeriana officinalis and 2 parts vodka in a glass jar.
Combine the root and vodka in the jar and seal it tightly with a lid.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and use 20 to 30 drops under the tongue as needed.

Steep 1 part of dried avena sativa plant material in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove plant material.
Transfer the herbal tincture to dark glass bottles with dropper lids and store in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

Store the mixture in a clean glass jar and steep it in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks.

Allow the tincture to mature for 4-6 weeks before using it to ensure optimal potency and flavor.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with eye twitching because it promotes improved blood flow to the eyes and brain. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 part of ginkgo biloba seeds on a dry sunny day.
Chop the ginkgo biloba leaves into fine pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped leaves and seeds in a clean glass jar with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking it daily for 30 seconds.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh or dried flowers when in full bloom and dry them for at least 2 days.

Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids and obtain a clear liquid.

Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass bottle to separate the tincture.
